title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship that teaches you how to write code that is readable and maintainable.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
description: One of the most significant books in software development, covering everything from personal responsibility to career development.

title: Refactoring
author: Martin Fowler
description: A guide to improving the design of existing code without changing its external behavior.

title: Design Patterns
author: Erich Gamma
description: The 'Gang of Four' book that introduced fundamental design patterns to the world of object-oriented programming.

title: Eloquent JavaScript
author: Marijn Haverbeke
description: A modern introduction to programming using JavaScript, covering the language, the browser, and Node.js.

title: You Don't Know JS: Up & Going
author: Kyle Simpson
description: An essential guide to the core mechanisms of the JavaScript language, starting from the very basics.

title: The Clean Architecture
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A guide to software structure and design, focusing on creating systems that are independent of frameworks and UI.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
author: Thomas H. Cormen
description: The definitive comprehensive guide to algorithms, used as a standard textbook in universities worldwide.

title: Grokking Algorithms
author: Aditya Bhargava
description: A fully illustrated, friendly guide that teaches you how to apply common algorithms to practical problems.

title: Test Driven Development
author: Kent Beck
description: The book that popularized TDD, showing how to write cleaner, more robust code through a test-first approach.

title: Domain-Driven Design
author: Eric Evans
description: A framework for making complex software projects manageable by connecting the implementation to a model of the business.

title: Code Complete
author: Steve McConnell
description: Widely considered one of the best practical guides to programming, covering construction and software quality.

title: Head First Design Patterns
author: Eric Freeman
description: A visually rich guide to design patterns that uses brain-friendly learning to help you understand software design.

title: Soft Skills
author: John Sonmez
description: A guide for software developers to manage their careers, fitness, and finances.

title: Cracking the Coding Interview
author: Gayle Laakmann McDowell
description: The most popular resource for preparing for software engineering interviews at top tech companies.

title: Effective Java
author: Joshua Bloch
description: Best practices for the Java platform, teaching you how to use the language's features effectively.

title: The Mythical Man-Month
author: Frederick Brooks
description: Classic essays on software engineering management and the complexities of large-scale projects.

title: Continuous Delivery
author: Jez Humble
description: How to create a reliable and automated pipeline for delivering software to users.

title: Modern Full-Stack Development
author: Frank Zammetti
description: A guide to building modern web applications using React, Node.js, and other popular tools.

title: Don't Make Me Think
author: Steve Krug
description: A common-sense guide to web usability, essential for any frontend or full-stack developer.

title: Working Effectively with Legacy Code
author: Michael Feathers
description: Strategies for working with and improving large codebases that lack tests.

title: Learning React
author: Alex Banks
description: A hands-on guide to building web applications with the React library.

title: Node.js Design Patterns
author: Mario Casciaro
description: Master the best practices and patterns for building robust Node.js applications.

title: Programming Pearls
author: Jon Bentley
description: A collection of essays that offer clever solutions to common programming problems.

title: The Art of Computer Programming
author: Donald Knuth
description: One of the most foundational works in computer science, covering algorithms and data structures.

title: User Story Mapping
author: Jeff Patton
description: A practical guide to understanding what users really want and how to build it.

title: Site Reliability Engineering
author: Niall Richard Murphy
description: How Google runs its production systems, focusing on reliability and scalability.

title: Building Microservices
author: Sam Newman
description: A comprehensive guide to designing, deploying, and maintaining microservices architectures.

title: The Phoenix Project
author: Gene Kim
description: A novel about IT, DevOps, and helping your business win through technical excellence.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: The best resource for understanding how to build scalable, reliable, and maintainable data systems.
